text.content: "Here's how to build it. First I count the bags. 1, 2. Two bags means 2 groups, so I place 2 in the first slot. Then I look inside one bag and count the items. 1, 2, 3, 4. Four items means 4 in each group, so I place 4 in the second slot. The answer is 2 times 4, because that's what the picture shows: 2 groups of 4."

rich_text.plain_text: "Here's how to build it. First I count the bags. 1, 2. Two bags means 2 groups, so I place 2 in the first slot. Then I look inside one bag and count the items. 1, 2, 3, 4. rich_text.plain_text: Numbers in expression highlight sequentially: first 5, then 6.

text.content: Option A demonstrates counting: circles pulse one by one showing 5 groups, then items in one circle highlight showing 6 in each.

rich_text.plain_text: Option A demonstrates counting: circles pulse one by one showing 5 groups, then items in one circle highlight showing 6 in each.

text.content: "Here's how to read 5 times 6. The first number is groups. I count the circles: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. Five groups. The second number is in each group. I count inside one circle: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6. Six in each. So 5 times 6 means 5 groups with 6 in each group. That's Option A."

rich_text.plain_text: "Here's how to read 5 times 6. The first number is groups. I count the circles: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. Five groups. The second number is in each group. I count inside one circle: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6. Six in each. So 5 times 6 means 5 groups with 6 in each group. That's Option A."
